missing_skills: Strong knowledge of C or C++
Deep Data Structures and Algorithms implementation proficiency
Object-Oriented Programming (OOP) principles demonstrated via C++/C code examples
Specific experience with low-level memory management/pointers common in C/C++ interviews
strengths: Demonstrated strong aptitude for complex technical concepts (DevOps, Cloud Architecture)
Experience with systematic processes like CI/CD and Infrastructure as Code
Possesses good foundational problem-solving abilities that are transferable to software development
Showcases a willingness to learn new technologies rapidly.
weaknesses: The resume heavily emphasizes DevOps and Cloud (Azure, Docker), which suggests a domain pivot away from core software engineering.
The core requirement of strong C/C++ knowledge is entirely absent or unproven on the resume.
Lack of specific project examples demonstrating complex Data Structures or Algorithms implementation.
recommendations: Crucially, if applying for this role (Software Engineer requiring C/C++/DSA), the candidate must restructure their profile to lead with core programming achievements.

Dedicate a new 'Core Programming Projects' section showcasing complex DSA implementations or OOP projects written in C++ (e.g., a linked list implementation, graph traversal, or small compiler/interpreter project).
If the goal is to transition into software development fundamentals, they must prepare aggressively for C++/DSA technical interview rounds and include proof of this study on their resume.
De-emphasize high-level DevOps tools (Jenkins, Kubernetes) if applying solely for a core SWE role that tests lower-level programming knowledge.
